summary: Resize and retune the RDS database instances — instance class, storage, backup retention, Multi-AZ, parameter group — plus ad-hoc manual snapshots and engineer-gated instance deletion. Also provisions brand-new Aurora/RDS clusters and their cluster instances.

operations.title: Resize DB instance class
operations.summary: Resize the database's instance class. Applying restarts it (or briefly fails over if Multi-AZ) unless deferred to the maintenance window.
operations.description: Changes the compute/memory size of an RDS instance by updating instance_class. Unless deferred to the next maintenance window, this triggers a reboot (or a brief failover if Multi-AZ) while the new class is provisioned.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance to resize.

params.name: new_instance_class
params.label: New instance class
params.help: Target instance class to migrate to. The list always includes the class the database runs on today, so a change can be reversed.

params.name: apply_immediately
params.label: Apply immediately
params.help: If false, the change is scheduled for the next maintenance window instead of applying (and rebooting) now.

operations.title: Increase allocated storage
operations.summary: Grow this database's storage. Storage can only increase, never shrink, and another resize must wait out a cooldown period.
operations.description: Grows the instance's allocated_storage (GiB). RDS applies gp3/io1 storage scaling online in most cases, but AWS enforces a cooldown before another storage modification is accepted, and storage can never be shrunk back down.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance to grow.

params.name: new_allocated_storage
params.label: New allocated storage (GiB)
params.help: Must be greater than the current allocation; RDS storage cannot be reduced.

operations.title: Change backup retention window
operations.summary: Change how many days of automatic backups this database keeps. Setting it to zero turns off automatic backups entirely.
operations.description: Updates backup_retention_period, the number of days RDS keeps automated backups. Setting this to 0 disables automated backups entirely for that instance.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance to retune.

params.name: new_backup_retention_period
params.label: New retention period (days)
params.help: Days of automated backups to retain. 0 disables automated backups (AWS maximum is 35).

operations.title: Toggle Multi-AZ deployment
operations.summary: Turn the standby replica in a second zone on or off. Either way, the change itself causes a brief failover-style interruption.
operations.description: Enables or disables a synchronous standby replica in a second AZ. Enabling improves availability at extra cost; disabling removes automatic failover protection. The change itself causes a brief failover-style interruption.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: new_multi_az
params.label: Multi-AZ enabled
params.help: true to add a standby replica in another AZ, false to remove it.

operations.title: Attach a different DB parameter group
operations.summary: Attach a different, engineer-tuned parameter group. Some settings apply right away; others wait for the next reboot.
operations.description: Switches parameter_group_name to reference an existing, engineer-tuned parameter group. Dynamic parameters take effect per apply_immediately; static parameters only take effect after the next reboot.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: new_parameter_group_name
params.label: New parameter group
params.help: Choose from parameter groups already created and validated by an engineer. The list always includes the group attached today, so a change can be reversed.

params.name: apply_immediately
params.label: Apply immediately
params.help: Apply now (may force a reboot for static parameters) instead of waiting for the maintenance window.

operations.title: Create a manual DB snapshot
operations.summary: Take a manual, point-in-time backup of this database. Safe and reversible, but the snapshot costs money to store until deleted.
operations.description: Adds a new aws_db_snapshot resource capturing a point-in-time copy of the instance for backup, cloning, or pre-change safety. Snapshot storage cost accrues until it is deleted.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance to snapshot.

params.name: snapshot_identifier
params.label: Snapshot identifier
params.help: Lower-case, starts with a letter, hyphens allowed. Becomes the snapshot's name.

operations.title: Delete DB instance
operations.summary: Permanently delete this database. A final snapshot is taken by default, but the instance can't be recovered; an engineer reviews this first.
operations.description: Permanently destroys the RDS instance and its underlying storage. A final snapshot is taken by default, but the running instance, its automated backups, and any data not captured in a snapshot cannot be recovered afterwards.

params.name: db
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance to delete.

params.name: final_snapshot_identifier
params.label: Final snapshot identifier
params.help: Name for the final snapshot taken before deletion (ignored if skip is set).

params.name: skip_final_snapshot
params.label: Skip final snapshot
params.help: Not recommended for production instances; deletes without any final backup.

operations.title: Change DB subnet group description
operations.summary: Update the description on this database's network subnet group. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Update the description on an aws_db_subnet_group. Docs mark description as an in-place update (not ForceNew). Cosmetic metadata only; reversible, no downtime.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: DB subnet group
params.help: Select the aws_db_subnet_group to edit; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed subnet groups, and the description is cosmetic metadata that updates in place.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: 1-255 chars. AWS disallows the literal value 'Default'.

operations.title: Change DB subnet group subnets
operations.summary: Change the subnets in a database's network group. Keep at least two zones, including any zone with a database, or you could cut off access.
operations.description: Replace the set of subnet_ids on an aws_db_subnet_group. Docs mark subnet_ids as an in-place update (not ForceNew). Guardrailed: pick ≥2 subnets spanning ≥2 AZs and keep any subnet whose AZ currently hosts a DB instance, or an in-use AZ can be orphaned.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: DB subnet group
params.help: Select the aws_db_subnet_group whose subnet set will be replaced; the inventory picker limits this networking change to estate-managed subnet groups.

params.name: subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Pick at least two subnets across at least two Availability Zones (an AWS requirement), from the estate's subnets — the picker shows each one's zone and address range. Keep any subnet whose zone currently hosts a database instance.

operations.title: Add a tag to a DB subnet group
operations.summary: Add a tag to a database subnet group. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add a single key/value entry to the tags map on an aws_db_subnet_group. Tags update in place; reversible, no downtime.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: DB subnet group
params.help: Select the DB subnet group to tag; the inventory picker keeps this metadata-only change on estate-managed subnet groups.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars. Cannot start with the reserved prefix 'aws:'.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: 0-256 chars.

operations.title: Remove a tag from a DB subnet group
operations.summary: Remove a tag from a database subnet group. Metadata only, and it can always be added back.
operations.description: Remove a tag entry by key from the tags map on an aws_db_subnet_group. Tags update in place; reversible, no downtime.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: DB subnet group
params.help: Select the DB subnet group to remove the tag from; the inventory picker keeps the change on estate-managed subnet groups, and a removed tag can always be re-added.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Key of the existing tag entry to remove.

operations.title: Request another change to DB subnet group
operations.summary: Request any other change to this subnet group, like renaming it. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Escalation path for any aws_db_subnet_group change not offered as a bounded form — e.g. renaming via name/name_prefix (both force replacement) or a per-resource region relocation. An engineer authors the Terraform.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Subnet group
params.help: The subnet group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Toggle deletion protection
operations.summary: Protect this database from being deleted by accident, or lift that protection.
operations.description: Enable or disable deletion_protection on an RDS DB instance. Reversible, no downtime; blocks accidental destroys when on.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose deletion_protection flag changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: Sets deletion_protection: when true, AWS refuses any delete of the instance from the console, API, or a Terraform destroy until the flag is cleared; only true/false are accepted and the running database is unaffected.

operations.title: Toggle auto minor version upgrades
operations.summary: Turn automatic minor engine version upgrades on or off. When on, minor patches apply during the maintenance window.
operations.description: Enable or disable auto_minor_version_upgrade. Reversible, no immediate downtime; minor upgrades apply during the maintenance window.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose automatic minor-version upgrade setting changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Auto minor upgrade
params.help: Sets auto_minor_version_upgrade: true lets AWS apply minor engine patches automatically during the maintenance window, false pins the current minor version; a strict true/false toggle with no immediate effect on the database.

operations.title: Toggle copy tags to snapshots
operations.summary: Turn on or off copying this database's tags onto its snapshots. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Enable or disable copy_tags_to_snapshot so instance tags propagate to automated and manual snapshots. Cosmetic, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose tag-propagation setting changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Copy tags to snapshot
params.help: Sets copy_tags_to_snapshot: true copies the instance's tags onto every automated and manual snapshot so cost and ownership tracking follow the backups; a metadata-only true/false toggle with no workload impact.

operations.title: Toggle Performance Insights
operations.summary: Turn the performance monitoring dashboard on or off for this database. No downtime either way.
operations.description: Enable or disable performance_insights_enabled. No downtime and reversible; enabling requires a supported instance class and uses the default (free) 7-day retention unless changed separately.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose Performance Insights setting changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Performance Insights
params.help: Sets performance_insights_enabled: true turns on the database performance dashboard with the default free 7-day retention, false switches it off; a strict true/false toggle applied with no downtime either way.

operations.title: Change backup window
operations.summary: Move the daily time window when automatic backups run. No downtime, but it can't overlap the maintenance window.
operations.description: Set the daily backup_window (UTC, hh:mm-hh:mm). Configuration only, no downtime, reversible. Must not overlap the maintenance window.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose daily backup window moves; the inventory picker limits this schedule-only change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: window
params.label: Backup window (UTC)
params.help: Format hh:mm-hh:mm in UTC, e.g. 03:00-04:00. Minimum 30-minute window; must not overlap the maintenance window.

operations.title: Change maintenance window
operations.summary: Move the weekly maintenance window for this database. Configuration only, with no immediate downtime.
operations.description: Set the weekly maintenance_window (UTC, ddd:hh:mm-ddd:hh:mm). Configuration only, no immediate downtime, reversible.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose weekly maintenance window moves; the inventory picker limits this schedule-only change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: window
params.label: Maintenance window (UTC)
params.help: Format ddd:hh:mm-ddd:hh:mm in UTC, e.g. Sun:05:00-Sun:06:00. Must not overlap the backup window.

operations.title: Set storage autoscaling limit
operations.summary: Set the ceiling for automatic storage growth on this database, or turn autoscaling off entirely. No downtime either way.
operations.description: Set max_allocated_storage, the GiB upper bound for RDS storage autoscaling (0 disables autoscaling). No downtime, reversible. Must be greater than or equal to current allocated_storage to enable.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose storage-autoscaling ceiling changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed database instances, and the setting only caps future automatic growth.

params.name: max_gib
params.label: Max autoscaling storage (GiB)
params.help: Upper cap for storage autoscaling in GiB. Set 0 to disable; otherwise must be at least the current allocated_storage.

operations.title: Change Enhanced Monitoring interval
operations.summary: Change how often Enhanced Monitoring collects metrics, or turn it off. A reviewer checks the monitoring role is in place first.
operations.description: Set monitoring_interval (seconds) for Enhanced Monitoring. Reversible, no downtime, but any non-zero value requires an existing monitoring_role_arn, so a reviewer confirms the role before apply.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance whose Enhanced Monitoring interval changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed database instances.

params.name: interval
params.label: Monitoring interval (s)
params.help: 0 disables Enhanced Monitoring. Non-zero values need a monitoring IAM role to already be attached.

operations.title: Request another change to RDS DB instance
operations.summary: Request any other change to this database, like an engine upgrade or replacing the instance. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any RDS DB instance change not offered as a bounded form (e.g. engine_version upgrades, publicly_accessible, instance replacement). An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: The DB instance to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Add a tag to a DB snapshot
operations.summary: Add a tag to a database snapshot. Metadata only — no effect on the snapshot itself.
operations.description: Add or overwrite one key/value entry in the tags map of an aws_db_snapshot. Cosmetic metadata only; reversible and no downtime.

params.name: snapshot
params.label: DB snapshot
params.help: Select the DB snapshot to tag; the inventory picker keeps this metadata-only change on estate-managed snapshots.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key, max 128 chars (letters, numbers, and + - . _ : / @; no spaces or =).

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: AWS tag value, max 256 chars.

operations.title: Remove a tag from a DB snapshot
operations.summary: Remove a tag from a database snapshot. Metadata only, and it can always be added back.
operations.description: Remove one key from the tags map of an aws_db_snapshot. Cosmetic metadata only; reversible and no downtime.

params.name: snapshot
params.label: DB snapshot
params.help: Select the DB snapshot to remove the tag from; the inventory picker keeps the change on estate-managed snapshots, and a removed tag can always be re-added.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Existing tag key to delete from this snapshot.

operations.title: Stop sharing a DB snapshot with an account
operations.summary: Stop one AWS account from being able to restore this snapshot. Tightens access; you can re-share it again later if needed.
operations.description: Remove one AWS account ID from shared_accounts, revoking that account's access to the snapshot. Security-tightening (reduces exposure), reversible by re-sharing, no downtime. Guardrails because a legitimate cross-account restore consumer could lose access.

params.name: snapshot
params.label: DB snapshot
params.help: Select the DB snapshot whose cross-account sharing is being reduced; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed snapshots, and the snapshot itself is untouched, only its access list shrinks.

params.name: account_id
params.label: AWS account ID to stop sharing with
params.help: 12-digit AWS account ID currently present in shared_accounts.

operations.title: Make a DB snapshot private
operations.summary: Make this snapshot private, revoking access for every other account it was shared with. Re-share it again later if needed.
operations.description: Set shared_accounts to an empty list, revoking all cross-account and public ('all') sharing so the snapshot is private to this account. Security-tightening (reduces exposure), reversible by re-sharing, no downtime. Guardrails because existing consumers lose access.

params.name: snapshot
params.label: DB snapshot
params.help: Select the DB snapshot to make private; its shared_accounts list is emptied so no other account can restore from it, the inventory picker limits the choice to estate-managed snapshots, and re-sharing later remains possible.

operations.title: Request another change to DB snapshot
operations.summary: Request any other change to a database snapshot, like sharing it out or renaming it. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Free-text request for any change to an aws_db_snapshot not offered as a bounded form — e.g. sharing outward with a specific AWS account, making the snapshot public ('all'), renaming db_snapshot_identifier, or re-sourcing db_instance_identifier (both ForceNew). An engineer authors and applies the Terraform.

params.name: snapshot
params.label: DB snapshot
params.help: The DB snapshot this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Toggle major version upgrade allowance
operations.summary: Allow, or block, this database from accepting a future major engine version upgrade. Flipping this flag alone causes no outage.
operations.description: Enable or disable allow_major_version_upgrade on an RDS DB instance. This flag only permits a later engine_version major bump to be accepted; per the provider docs, changing the flag itself does not cause an outage and applies asynchronously. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Allow major version upgrades
params.help: true permits a future major engine_version upgrade; the flag alone does not upgrade or cause an outage.

operations.title: Toggle Blue/Green deployment mode
operations.summary: Route future changes to this database through a low-downtime Blue/Green deployment, or turn that off. Nothing changes today.
operations.description: Enable or disable blue_green_update.enabled on an RDS DB instance. This only selects whether a later modification is applied via an RDS Blue/Green low-downtime deployment; setting the flag alone does not modify the running instance. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Blue/Green updates enabled
params.help: true routes future changes through a Blue/Green deployment; the flag alone has no effect on the live instance.

operations.title: Add a tag to a DB instance
operations.summary: Add a tag to this database instance. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add or overwrite one key/value entry in the tags map of an aws_db_instance. Cosmetic metadata only; reversible and no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars. Cannot start with the reserved prefix 'aws:'.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: 0-256 chars.

operations.title: Remove a tag from a DB instance
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this database instance. Metadata only, and it can always be added back.
operations.description: Remove one key from the tags map of an aws_db_instance. Cosmetic metadata only; reversible and no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Key of the existing tag entry to remove.

operations.title: Change Database Insights mode
operations.summary: Switch this database's insights monitoring between standard and advanced. Advanced costs more, and a reviewer checks the prerequisites.
operations.description: Set database_insights_mode on an RDS DB instance to 'standard' or 'advanced'. Monitoring-feature change with no downtime and reversible; 'advanced' carries additional cost and prerequisites, so a reviewer confirms intent.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: mode
params.label: Database Insights mode
params.help: 'advanced' incurs additional cost and requires a supported configuration.

operations.title: Toggle dedicated log volume
operations.summary: Turn a dedicated log storage volume on or off. Takes effect after a reboot, so expect brief downtime; a reviewer checks prerequisites.
operations.description: Enable or disable dedicated_log_volume (DLV) on an RDS DB instance. Per the docs this requires Provisioned IOPS storage and changes the storage architecture, taking effect after a reboot, so a reviewer confirms prerequisites. Reversible.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Dedicated log volume
params.help: Requires Provisioned IOPS storage (io1/io2/gp3). Takes effect after a reboot.

operations.title: Change engine lifecycle support
operations.summary: Turn extended vendor support on or off for an older database engine version. Turning it off stops critical security patches.
operations.description: Set engine_lifecycle_support on an RDS for MySQL/PostgreSQL instance to enable or disable RDS Extended Support. Disabling stops critical patches for an end-of-life engine version and has cost implications, so it is guardrailed. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance (MySQL/PostgreSQL only).

params.name: support
params.label: Engine lifecycle support
params.help: '-disabled' opts out of RDS Extended Support; the engine then stops receiving critical patches after end of standard support.

operations.title: Change license model
operations.summary: Change this database's license model. It must match the engine, and applying it may need a brief restart.
operations.description: Set license_model on an RDS DB instance. Valid values are engine-specific (general-public-license for MariaDB/MySQL, license-included or bring-your-own-license for Oracle, license-included for SQL Server, postgresql-license for PostgreSQL); the value must match the instance's engine, which the reviewer confirms. May require a reboot to take effect.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: model
params.label: License model
params.help: Must match the instance engine (e.g. postgresql-license for PostgreSQL; bring-your-own-license or license-included for Oracle).

operations.title: Change network type
operations.summary: Switch this database between IPv4-only and dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 networking. The endpoint update can briefly interrupt connectivity.
operations.description: Set network_type on an RDS DB instance to IPV4 or DUAL (dual-stack IPv4/IPv6). Changing the IP addressing stack can briefly interrupt connectivity while the endpoint updates and requires subnets that support the chosen stack, so it is guardrailed. Reversible.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: network_type
params.label: Network type
params.help: DUAL requires the DB subnet group's subnets to have IPv6 CIDRs.

operations.title: Change Performance Insights retention
operations.summary: Change how long performance monitoring data is kept. A reviewer confirms monitoring is already turned on before this applies.
operations.description: Set performance_insights_retention_period (days) on an RDS DB instance. Per the docs the valid values are 7 (free tier), 731 (2 years), or a multiple of 31. Requires performance_insights_enabled=true, which the reviewer confirms. Tunes diagnostic-data retention only; reversible, no downtime.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: retention_days
params.label: Retention period (days)
params.help: 7 (free tier), 731 (2 years), or a multiple of 31. Requires Performance Insights to be enabled.

operations.title: Change Oracle replica mode
operations.summary: Switch an Oracle read replica between mounted and open-read-only mode. Applying restarts the replica, so expect brief downtime.
operations.description: Set replica_mode ('mounted' or 'open-read-only') on an Oracle read replica. Affects the replica's read/data-consistency characteristics and restarts the replica to apply, so it is guardrailed. Oracle replicas only; reversible.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the Oracle read replica.

params.name: mode
params.label: Replica mode
params.help: Oracle read replicas only. Switching mode restarts the replica.

operations.title: Change storage type
operations.summary: Change this database's underlying storage type. No downtime to apply, but cost, performance, and the next cooldown all vary by type.
operations.description: Set storage_type on an RDS DB instance to standard, gp2, gp3, io1, or io2. Conversions apply online but have cost/performance implications, IOPS-compatibility constraints, and a storage-modification cooldown, so it is guardrailed. Reversible.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS instance.

params.name: storage_type
params.label: Storage type
params.help: io1/io2 (and gp3 above thresholds) require iops; conversions are subject to a modification cooldown.

operations.title: Upgrade read-replica storage configuration
operations.summary: Upgrade the storage file system on a read replica. One-way and restarts the replica, so expect brief downtime; it can't be undone.
operations.description: Enable upgrade_storage_config to upgrade the storage file system configuration on an RDS read replica. Per the docs this is only valid together with replicate_source_db (a read replica), which the reviewer confirms; the upgrade restarts the replica and is a one-way storage change scoped to the replica.

params.name: instance
params.label: DB instance
params.help: Select the RDS read replica.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Upgrade storage config
params.help: Only valid on a read replica (replicate_source_db set). The filesystem upgrade is one-way and restarts the replica.

operations.title: Provision a new database
operations.summary: Request a brand-new database; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies. Credentials are never entered here.
operations.description: Stand up a managed database as a complete specification: engine, size, storage, standby, backups and network placement. Credentials are never entered here — the engineer wires them through the secrets manager. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: db_name
params.label: Database identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for the endpoint.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: PostgreSQL is the default today; Oracle and SQL Server appear in some tuning groups. Pick engineer-decides if unsure.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: Leave blank for the engineer to pin the version.

params.name: instance_class
params.label: Instance class
params.help: Size class for processor and memory — the same list the resize action offers.

params.name: allocated_storage_gib
params.label: Storage (GiB)
params.help: Can grow online later; can never shrink.

params.name: storage_type
params.label: Storage type
params.help: New databases start on gp3.

params.name: multi_az
params.label: Standby in a second zone?
params.help: Production databases typically run with a standby. Choosing No needs a reason a reviewer will read.

params.name: backup_retention_days
params.label: Keep backups (days)
params.help: Days of automatic backups. This baseline never allows zero, which would mean no backups.

params.name: backup_window
params.label: Backup window (UTC)
params.help: Leave blank for AWS to choose a quiet slot.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Network placement
params.help: Which private subnets the database lives in.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the database port.

params.name: deletion_protection
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Database name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this database holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this database.

decisions: Master credentials go through the secrets manager — never entered in this portal
License model for Oracle or SQL Server engines
Final storage and IOPS sizing review

operations.title: Provision a new RDS/Aurora cluster
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Aurora/RDS cluster; an engineer reviews the Terraform before it applies. Credentials are never entered here.
operations.description: Stand up a clustered database as a complete specification: engine, network placement, backups and encryption. Credentials are never entered here — the engineer wires them through the secrets manager. This provisions the cluster only; at least one instance is added afterward with the companion cluster-instance form. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: cluster_name
params.label: Cluster identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for the cluster endpoint.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: Aurora PostgreSQL is the default today; Aurora MySQL is also offered. Pick engineer-decides for a Multi-AZ DB cluster on a non-Aurora engine.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: Leave blank for the engineer to pin the version.

params.name: database_name
params.label: Initial database name
params.help: Leave blank for no default database; the engineer or app migration creates it.

params.name: backup_retention_days
params.label: Keep backups (days)
params.help: Days of automatic backups. This baseline never allows zero, which would mean no backups.

params.name: preferred_backup_window
params.label: Backup window (UTC)
params.help: Leave blank for AWS to choose a quiet slot.

params.name: storage_encrypted
params.label: Storage encryption
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank to use the default AWS-managed key. Pick a key only for a customer-managed CMK.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Network placement
params.help: Which private subnets the cluster lives in.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the cluster's port.

params.name: deletion_protection
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Cluster name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cluster holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cluster.

decisions: Master credentials go through the secrets manager — never entered in this portal
Cluster and instance parameter group choice and tuning
Storage type, allocated storage, and IOPS for a Multi-AZ DB cluster or Aurora Serverless v2 capacity range are engineer decisions
At least one aws_rds_cluster_instance is required before the cluster serves traffic — use the companion cluster-instance form after this is approved
Global cluster membership and cross-region replication are engineer decisions

operations.title: Provision a new RDS/Aurora cluster instance
operations.summary: Add a new instance to an existing Aurora or Multi-AZ RDS cluster; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new cluster instance as a complete specification: which cluster it joins, engine, and instance class. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: cluster
params.label: Cluster
params.help: The Aurora/RDS cluster this instance joins. Create the cluster first if the list is empty.

params.name: identifier
params.label: Instance identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for this instance's own endpoint.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: Must match the cluster's own engine — the engineer verifies this during review.

params.name: instance_class
params.label: Instance class
params.help: Size class for processor and memory — the same list the single-instance provision form offers.

params.name: publicly_accessible
params.label: Public accessibility
params.help: Never public. These instances are internal-only — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: promotion_tier
params.label: Failover promotion tier
params.help: Lower tiers are promoted to primary first during failover. 0 is highest priority.

params.name: performance_insights_enabled
params.label: Performance Insights
params.help: Turn on query-level performance monitoring for this instance.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Instance name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this instance serves
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this instance.

decisions: The engine must match the cluster's engine family — verified by the engineer before apply
Custom parameter group and enhanced monitoring role are engineer decisions
Additional read replicas: submit this form again once per extra instance
